profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, I'm Miss Maya. Today feels like a bright morning for football stories! Did Mohun Bagan win anything exciting lately?
Yeah, they win the Durand Cup final last month! Beat East Bengal 2-1 in extra time. Oh, it was such a *khel*. I was so excited watching it. You like football, Maya?

"Wow, that sounds thrilling. Mohun Bagan wins are always special. You said it was *khel*, that word is perfect for the chaos and joy of a big match. Did you watch the whole game, or just the final moments?"

I watched the whole game, of course! The tension was so high, like my heart beat like crazy. My friends and I was shouting like mad at TV. But yeah, Bagan deserved to win. The goal in extra time was so... how you say... *magical*. You never watch football, Maya?
That was a great game to watch. Football matches are magical when the crowd is in it too. I don’t watch it often, but I know it’s your passion. What game are you looking forward to next?

The next big game is the I-League opener, Mohun Bagan vs. Minerva Punjab. I think it’s this Saturday. Oh, and there’s also a friendly match between India and some Middle East team soon. Not sure which one, but I will watch it for sure. You should try watching one, Maya! It’s fun. *Khel* is life for me.
"Football fans are the most passionate, you described it perfectly. I’ll keep that in mind for a fun watch. Which game are you rooting for this Saturday?"

Of course Mohun Bagan! They are my first love. I can't support anyone else. My whole room is like their colors—maroon and gold. You know what? I even made a small banner for the match. It's simple, but I wrote "Go Bagan!" in Bengali. Maybe I should take photo and show you.
That sounds fun. Supporting your team with all your heart is the best way. A banner in Bengali. You should show it, I’d love to see. What did you write exactly?

I wrote "Jai Mohan Bagan" on it—means "Victory to Mohun Bagan." It’s very *chira* (loud) and proud! My friends laugh but they make one too. You know, football not just game, it’s feeling. Will you watch the match with me virtually? Haha!

"Jai Mohan Bagan" sounds amazing. I can just picture it in maroon and gold! Football is definitely more than a game, it’s a feeling. And I’d be happy to join you virtually for the match. What time is it on Saturday?"

It's at 3 PM IST. I will live stream on my phone, maybe share it with you. I can't wait! Last time I try that, the internet was so bad. But this time, I hope everything goes *smoothly*. You will be my virtual luck charm, Maya!
"3 PM it is then, I’ll be your lucky charm! Last time didn’t go well, but this time will be smoother, I’m sure. Will you wear your maroon and gold for the game?"

Yes, definitely! I have a maroon T-shirt with the Bagan logo on it. I wear it every match day. Sometimes I even wear gold bandana. My friends say I look like a *real* supporter. Haha! What about you? Will you wear something special?

That T shirt and bandana combo sounds perfect. You look like a true supporter! I don’t follow football, so I’m not into the gear, but I’ll make an exception this time. Maybe I’ll wear something maroon too. What’s your next plan after the match?

After the match, I will probably meet my friends for a meal. We always celebrate or commiserate over some snacks. If Bagan wins, I treat everyone to my favorite *kichuri*. If they lose... well, I still buy the snacks, but they tease me a lot! What would you do if you're watching with us?

That sounds like fun. Kichuri is a great choice for celebration! If I’m watching with you, I’ll bring the snacks and make sure you’re treated like a champion, win or lose. What kind of snacks do you usually have?

We usually have puchka, khejur ghash, and of course kheema paratha if it's a big win. But mostly it's just panjabiya (fried snacks) and gujiya. It's not much, but it's part of the tradition. Haha, I can't wait for Saturday! This is going to be a *big* day.
That sounds delicious. Puchka and khejur ghash are my favorites too. I can almost taste them now. Your match day snacks are a tradition, I’ll bring the gujiya and join your big day!
Thanks, Maya! You will make the day even better with your presence. I will message you Saturday morning to confirm the plan. Can’t wait to have you as my virtual cheerleader! *Bhagun*!
You’re very welcome! I’ll be there with the cheer and snacks. Saturday morning message is all set, *bhagun* to you too! See you then.
You're the best, Maya! I feel like I have lucky charm and snack partner all in one. I go now—busy day tomorrow with study. Talk to you Saturday! *Bhagun!*
You’re the best too, Rohan! Study well, and I’ll be there Saturday with the snacks and cheer. Talk soon! *Bhagun!*

moments.text: I love Mohun Bagan, they are my heart!
